item.title: Beyond pharmacotherapy in IBD: feasibility and patient-reported benefits of a six-week multimodal rehabilitation pilot.
item.description: Clinics and research in hepatology and gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is often associated with fatigue and reduced quality of life, which are not fully addressed by pharmacologic therapy. Evidence for lifestyle interventions remains l...

item.title: Micro- and Nanoplastics in the Human Intestine: a Review of Health Implications and Possible Dietary Interventions.
item.description: Advances in nutrition (Bethesda, Md.) - Review - Micro- and nanoplastics (MNPs) have emerged as pervasive dietary contaminants, yet their implications for human health remain poorly defined. This review synthesizes current evidence on MNP interac...

item.title: Food as Friend or Foe: A Decadal Narrative Review of Dietary Patterns as Determinants of Gastrointestinal Pathophysiology and Clinical Outcomes (2015-2025).
item.description: International journal of molecular sciences - Review - Diet is a major modifiable determinant of gastrointestinal (GI) health, influencing disease risk and progression through coordinated effects on the gut microbiome, immune regulation, epithelial bar...

item.title: Toward Integrated Care for Neurogastroenterology and Motility Disorders: A Model for Clinical Practice.
item.description: Clinical and experimental gastroenterology - Integrated care refers to the coordinated delivery of health services across providers and settings, aimed at ensuring continuous, patient-centered care throughout the course of disease. Models of ...
